# Data Model — MyGarden

This document describes the data model for the MyGarden application. The primary data store is MongoDB, but the model is described in a storage-agnostic way to support future migration. See [architecture.md](./architecture.md) for how the abstract repository layer wraps data access.

**ID strategy:** All entity IDs are UUID v4, generated by the application layer (not the database). This decouples the identity scheme from MongoDB's UUID and makes future migration to PostgreSQL or other stores straightforward. In MongoDB, UUIDs are stored as strings in the `_id` field.

---

## 1. Entity Relationship Overview

```
AllowedEmail ──── gates registration ────► User
                                           │
                                           │ many-to-many (via GardenMembership)
                                           ▼
                                       Garden ──────── Season
                                           │               │
                                           │               │ (scoped by season)
                                           ▼               ▼
                                         Area ────── Planting ────── ActivityLog
                                           │               │
                                           │               │
                                           ▼               ▼
                                         Note            Task
                                           ▲
                                           │
                                     PlantProfile ◄──── Planting
                                           │
                                          File (attachments, referenced from logs/notes/profiles)
```

**Key relationships:**

- An **AllowedEmail** entry must exist before a **User** can register.
- A **User** can be a member of many **Gardens** (many-to-many via **GardenMembership**).
- A **Garden** has many **Areas** and many **Seasons**.
- A **Planting** ties a **PlantProfile** to an **Area** within a **Season**.
- **ActivityLogs**, **Tasks**, and **Notes** belong to a garden and are scoped to a season.
- **Files** are standalone records referencing Object Storage; linked from logs, notes, or profiles.

---

## 2. Collections / Entities

### 2.1 User

Represents a registered user account.


| Field          | Type   | Required | Description                                           |
| -------------- | ------ | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| `_id`          | UUID   | yes      | Primary key (v4, application-generated)               |
| `email`        | string | yes      | Unique, lowercase, used for login                     |
| `passwordHash` | string | yes      | bcrypt hash                                           |
| `displayName`  | string | yes      | Shown in UI and activity logs                         |
| `language`     | string | yes      | Preferred language (`"nb"` or `"en"`), default `"nb"` |
| `createdAt`    | Date   | auto     | Account creation timestamp                            |
| `updatedAt`    | Date   | auto     | Last profile update                                   |


**Indexes:**

- Unique index on `email`

---

### 2.2 AllowedEmail

Controls which email addresses are permitted to register. The app owner manages this list; registration is rejected for any email not present here.


| Field          | Type   | Required | Description                                                             |
| -------------- | ------ | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `_id`          | UUID   | yes      | Primary key (v4, application-generated)                                 |
| `email`        | string | yes      | Lowercase email address that is allowed to register                     |
| `addedBy`      | UUID   | no       | Ref → User who added this entry (null if seeded)                        |
| `registeredAt` | Date   | no       | Set when the email has been used to register (tracks invitation status) |
| `createdAt`    | Date   | auto     | When the entry was added to the allowlist                               |


**Indexes:**

- Unique index on `email`

**Rules:**

- During registration, the auth service queries this collection. If no matching document exists, registration is rejected.
- Once a user registers with the email, `registeredAt` is set. This allows the UI to show which invitations have been accepted.
- Allowlist entries can be managed via CLI seed script, or the `/admin/allowed-emails` API endpoints (restricted to the app owner).

---

### 2.3 Garden

Represents a single garden. The top-level entity that contains areas, seasons, and everything else. The data model supports multiple gardens per user from day one, even though the MVP UI may only expose a single garden.


| Field            | Type   | Required | Description                                    |
| ---------------- | ------ | -------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| `_id`            | UUID   | yes      | Primary key (v4, application-generated)        |
| `name`           | string | yes      | User-given name (e.g., "Home garden")          |
| `gridWidth`      | number | yes      | Grid width in cells (e.g., 10)                 |
| `gridHeight`     | number | yes      | Grid height in cells (e.g., 12)                |
| `cellSizeMeters` | number | yes      | Real-world size of one grid cell (default 1.0) |
| `createdBy`      | UUID   | yes      | Ref → User who created the garden              |
| `createdAt`      | Date   | auto     |                                                |
| `updatedAt`      | Date   | auto     |                                                |


**Indexes:**

- Index on `createdBy` (list gardens for a user, supplemented by GardenMembership)

---

### 2.4 GardenMembership

Join entity between User and Garden. Enables collaboration and multiple gardens per account.


| Field       | Type   | Required | Description                             |
| ----------- | ------ | -------- | --------------------------------------- |
| `_id`       | UUID   | yes      | Primary key (v4, application-generated) |
| `gardenId`  | UUID   | yes      | Ref → Garden                            |
| `userId`    | UUID   | yes      | Ref → User                              |
| `role`      | string | yes      | `"owner"` or `"member"`                 |
| `createdAt` | Date   | auto     | When the user joined                    |


**Indexes:**

- Unique compound index on `(gardenId, userId)`
- Index on `userId` (look up gardens for a user)
- Index on `gardenId` (look up members of a garden)

**Rules:**

- Every garden has exactly one owner (the creator).
- Members are added by the owner (post-MVP collaboration feature).
- In MVP, the creator is automatically the sole owner/member.

---

### 2.5 Season

Represents a growing season within a garden. Plantings, logs, tasks, and notes are scoped to a season.


| Field       | Type    | Required | Description                                |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| `_id`       | UUID    | yes      | Primary key (v4, application-generated)    |
| `gardenId`  | UUID    | yes      | Ref → Garden                               |
| `name`      | string  | yes      | User-given name (e.g., "2026")             |
| `startDate` | Date    | yes      | Season start (default: Jan 1)              |
| `endDate`   | Date    | yes      | Season end (default: Dec 31)               |
| `isActive`  | boolean | yes      | Whether this is the current working season |
| `createdAt` | Date    | auto     |                                            |
| `updatedAt` | Date    | auto     |                                            |


**Indexes:**

- Index on `gardenId`
- Unique compound index on `(gardenId, name)` (no duplicate season names per garden)

**Rules:**

- Only one season per garden can have `isActive: true` at a time.
- Archiving a season sets `isActive: false` and creates a new active season.

---

### 2.6 Area

A rectangular zone on the garden grid. Represents a raised bed, open bed, tree zone, path, etc.


| Field        | Type   | Required | Description                                                                        |
| ------------ | ------ | -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `_id`        | UUID   | yes      | Primary key (v4, application-generated)                                            |
| `gardenId`   | UUID   | yes      | Ref → Garden                                                                       |
| `name`       | string | yes      | User-given label (e.g., "Pallekarme 1")                                            |
| `type`       | string | yes      | One of: `"raised_bed"`, `"open_bed"`, `"tree_zone"`, `"path"`, `"lawn"`, `"other"` |
| `color`      | string | yes      | Hex color code for map rendering (e.g., `"#8B4513"`)                               |
| `gridX`      | number | yes      | Top-left cell X coordinate (0-based)                                               |
| `gridY`      | number | yes      | Top-left cell Y coordinate (0-based)                                               |
| `gridWidth`  | number | yes      | Width in grid cells                                                                |
| `gridHeight` | number | yes      | Height in grid cells                                                               |
| `createdAt`  | Date   | auto     |                                                                                    |
| `updatedAt`  | Date   | auto     |                                                                                    |


**Indexes:**

- Index on `gardenId`

**Notes:**

- Areas are season-independent — the physical layout of the garden persists across seasons.
- If the user restructures their garden between seasons, they edit the areas directly. Historical season data retains references to area IDs, so renamed/resized areas are still linked.

---

### 2.7 PlantProfile

A reusable plant template created by the user. Describes a type of plant (e.g., "Cherry Tomato") with its growing characteristics.


| Field                 | Type                             | Required | Description                                                                        |
| --------------------- | -------------------------------- | -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `_id`                 | UUID                             | yes      | Primary key (v4, application-generated)                                            |
| `userId`              | UUID                             | yes      | Ref → User (owner/creator)                                                         |
| `name`                | string                           | yes      | Plant name (e.g., "Cherry Tomato")                                                 |
| `type`                | string                           | yes      | One of: `"vegetable"`, `"herb"`, `"flower"`, `"berry"`, `"tree_shrub"`             |
| `notes`               | string                           | no       | Free-text notes                                                                    |
| `sowingWindowIndoor`  | `{ start: number, end: number }` | no       | Week numbers for indoor sowing window                                              |
| `sowingWindowOutdoor` | `{ start: number, end: number }` | no       | Week numbers for outdoor sowing window                                             |
| `sunRequirement`      | string                           | no       | `"full_sun"`, `"partial_shade"`, or `"shade"`                                      |
| `waterNeed`           | string                           | no       | `"low"`, `"medium"`, or `"high"`                                                   |
| `spacingPlantCm`      | number                           | no       | cm between plants                                                                  |
| `spacingRowCm`        | number                           | no       | cm between rows                                                                    |
| `daysToGermination`   | number                           | no       | Typical days from sowing to germination                                            |
| `daysToHarvest`       | number                           | no       | Typical days from transplant/sowing to harvest                                     |
| `companionPlants`     | string[]                         | no       | Names or IDs of compatible plants                                                  |
| `antagonistPlants`    | string[]                         | no       | Names or IDs of incompatible plants                                                |
| `cropRotationGroup`   | string                           | no       | e.g., `"legume"`, `"brassica"`, `"nightshade"`, `"root"`, `"allium"`, `"cucurbit"` |
| `createdAt`           | Date                             | auto     |                                                                                    |
| `updatedAt`           | Date                             | auto     |                                                                                    |


**Indexes:**

- Index on `userId`
- Index on `(userId, name)` for search/autocomplete

**Notes:**

- Plant profiles are owned by a user, not a garden. This allows the same profile to be reused across multiple gardens (future feature).
- Extended fields are all optional — MVP only requires `name`, `type`, and `notes`.

---

### 2.8 Planting

A concrete instance of a plant in a specific area during a specific season. This is the core planning entity.


| Field                | Type   | Required | Description                                         |
| -------------------- | ------ | -------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| `_id`                | UUID   | yes      | Primary key (v4, application-generated)             |
| `gardenId`           | UUID   | yes      | Ref → Garden                                        |
| `seasonId`           | UUID   | yes      | Ref → Season                                        |
| `areaId`             | UUID   | yes      | Ref → Area                                          |
| `plantProfileId`     | UUID   | no       | Ref → PlantProfile (null if entered ad-hoc)         |
| `plantName`          | string | yes      | Denormalized plant name (survives profile deletion) |
| `sowingMethod`       | string | yes      | `"indoor"` or `"direct_outdoor"`                    |
| `indoorSowDate`      | Date   | no       | Required if `sowingMethod` = `"indoor"`             |
| `transplantDate`     | Date   | no       | Required if `sowingMethod` = `"indoor"`             |
| `outdoorSowDate`     | Date   | no       | Required if `sowingMethod` = `"direct_outdoor"`     |
| `harvestWindowStart` | Date   | no       | Expected harvest start                              |
| `harvestWindowEnd`   | Date   | no       | Expected harvest end                                |
| `quantity`           | number | no       | Number of plants or seeds (optional)                |
| `notes`              | string | no       | Planting-specific notes                             |
| `createdBy`          | UUID   | yes      | Ref → User who created this planting                |
| `createdAt`          | Date   | auto     |                                                     |
| `updatedAt`          | Date   | auto     |                                                     |


**Indexes:**

- Compound index on `(gardenId, seasonId)` (primary query pattern)
- Index on `areaId`
- Index on `plantProfileId`

**Notes:**

- `plantName` is denormalized from the PlantProfile so that planting records remain meaningful even if the profile is later deleted or renamed.
- Date fields are conditionally required based on `sowingMethod`. Validation enforced at the service layer.

---

### 2.9 ActivityLog

A timestamped record of something the user did in the garden.


| Field             | Type   | Required | Description                                                                                                                              |
| ----------------- | ------ | -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `_id`             | UUID   | yes      | Primary key (v4, application-generated)                                                                                                  |
| `gardenId`        | UUID   | yes      | Ref → Garden                                                                                                                             |
| `seasonId`        | UUID   | yes      | Ref → Season                                                                                                                             |
| `plantingId`      | UUID   | no       | Ref → Planting (null if logged against an area directly)                                                                                 |
| `areaId`          | UUID   | no       | Ref → Area (null if logged against a planting)                                                                                           |
| `activity`        | string | yes      | One of: `"sown_indoors"`, `"sown_outdoors"`, `"transplanted"`, `"watered"`, `"fertilized"`, `"pruned"`, `"harvested"`, `"problem_noted"` |
| `date`            | Date   | yes      | When the activity occurred                                                                                                               |
| `note`            | string | no       | Free-text details                                                                                                                        |
| `quantity`        | number | no       | Amount harvested (kg, units) — relevant for `"harvested"`                                                                                |
| `fileIds`         | UUID[] | no       | Refs → File (attached photos)                                                                                                            |
| `createdBy`       | UUID   | yes      | Ref → User who logged this                                                                                                               |
| `clientTimestamp` | Date   | yes      | Timestamp from the client device (used for last-write-wins conflict resolution)                                                          |
| `createdAt`       | Date   | auto     | Server-side creation time                                                                                                                |


**Indexes:**

- Compound index on `(gardenId, seasonId, date)` (primary query: timeline of a season)
- Index on `plantingId`
- Index on `areaId`
- Index on `createdBy`

**Notes:**

- Either `plantingId` or `areaId` (or both) should be set. Validation enforced at the service layer.
- `clientTimestamp` is separate from `createdAt` to support offline-first logging. The client records when the user performed the action; the server records when it received it. Conflict resolution uses `clientTimestamp`.

---

### 2.10 Task

A to-do item, either auto-generated from the planting plan or manually created.


| Field         | Type   | Required | Description                                       |
| ------------- | ------ | -------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| `_id`         | UUID   | yes      | Primary key (v4, application-generated)           |
| `gardenId`    | UUID   | yes      | Ref → Garden                                      |
| `seasonId`    | UUID   | yes      | Ref → Season                                      |
| `plantingId`  | UUID   | no       | Ref → Planting (null for manual tasks)            |
| `areaId`      | UUID   | no       | Ref → Area                                        |
| `title`       | string | yes      | Task description (e.g., "Sow tomatoes indoors")   |
| `dueDate`     | Date   | yes      | When the task should be done                      |
| `source`      | string | yes      | `"auto"` (generated from plan) or `"manual"`      |
| `status`      | string | yes      | `"pending"`, `"done"`, or `"skipped"`             |
| `completedAt` | Date   | no       | When the task was marked done                     |
| `completedBy` | UUID   | no       | Ref → User who completed it                       |
| `linkedLogId` | UUID   | no       | Ref → ActivityLog created when task was completed |
| `createdAt`   | Date   | auto     |                                                   |
| `updatedAt`   | Date   | auto     |                                                   |


**Indexes:**

- Compound index on `(gardenId, seasonId, status, dueDate)` (primary query: upcoming tasks)
- Index on `plantingId`

**Notes:**

- Auto-generated tasks are created when a planting is saved. Changing a planting's dates regenerates its tasks.
- Completing a task can optionally create a linked ActivityLog entry (e.g., marking "Sow tomatoes indoors" as done creates a `sown_indoors` log).

---

### 2.11 Note

Free-text notes attached to a plant, area, or the season in general.


| Field        | Type   | Required | Description                                                |
| ------------ | ------ | -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
| `_id`        | UUID   | yes      | Primary key (v4, application-generated)                    |
| `gardenId`   | UUID   | yes      | Ref → Garden                                               |
| `seasonId`   | UUID   | yes      | Ref → Season                                               |
| `targetType` | string | yes      | `"planting"`, `"area"`, or `"season"`                      |
| `targetId`   | UUID   | no       | Ref → Planting or Area (null if `targetType` = `"season"`) |
| `content`    | string | yes      | Note text (markdown supported)                             |
| `fileIds`    | UUID[] | no       | Refs → File (attached images)                              |
| `createdBy`  | UUID   | yes      | Ref → User                                                 |
| `createdAt`  | Date   | auto     |                                                            |
| `updatedAt`  | Date   | auto     |                                                            |


**Indexes:**

- Compound index on `(gardenId, seasonId, targetType, targetId)`
- Index on `createdBy`

---

### 2.12 File

Metadata record for a file stored in Scaleway Object Storage. The actual binary lives in the S3 bucket; this document tracks it.


| Field              | Type   | Required | Description                                                    |
| ------------------ | ------ | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `_id`              | UUID   | yes      | Primary key (v4, application-generated)                        |
| `userId`           | UUID   | yes      | Ref → User who uploaded                                        |
| `gardenId`         | UUID   | yes      | Ref → Garden                                                   |
| `storageKey`       | string | yes      | S3 object key (e.g., `uploads/{userId}/{gardenId}/{uuid}.jpg`) |
| `originalFilename` | string | yes      | Original filename from the upload                              |
| `mimeType`         | string | yes      | MIME type (e.g., `image/jpeg`)                                 |
| `sizeBytes`        | number | yes      | File size in bytes                                             |
| `createdAt`        | Date   | auto     |                                                                |


**Indexes:**

- Index on `(userId, gardenId)`
- Index on `storageKey` (unique)

**Notes:**

- Deleting a File record should also delete the object from S3 (handled by the file service).
- The API never exposes the raw S3 key to the client. Instead, it generates a time-limited signed URL on read requests.

---

## 3. Conflict Resolution

The offline-first design uses **last-write-wins (LWW)** by `clientTimestamp`:

1. Every mutation from the client includes a `clientTimestamp` field set by the device clock at the time of the user action.
2. When the server receives a mutation (either live or from the offline sync queue), it compares `clientTimestamp` against the existing document's `updatedAt`.
3. If `clientTimestamp > updatedAt`, the write is applied and `updatedAt` is set to `clientTimestamp`.
4. If `clientTimestamp <= updatedAt`, the write is discarded (a newer change already exists).
5. The server returns the current document state in both cases so the client can reconcile.

This approach is simple, predictable, and sufficient for the expected usage pattern (a small number of family members with low write contention).

---

## 4. Query Patterns

The data model is optimized for the following primary query patterns:


| Query                       | Pattern                                                             | Indexes Used                                      |
| --------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| List all gardens for a user | Find GardenMemberships by `userId`, then fetch Gardens              | `GardenMembership.userId`                         |
| Load garden with areas      | Fetch Garden by `_id`, then Areas by `gardenId`                     | `Area.gardenId`                                   |
| Load season dashboard       | Fetch Plantings, Tasks, and Logs by `(gardenId, seasonId)`          | Compound indexes on each                          |
| Calendar view               | Fetch Tasks by `(gardenId, seasonId, status, dueDate)` range        | `Task.(gardenId, seasonId, status, dueDate)`      |
| Activity timeline           | Fetch Logs by `(gardenId, seasonId, date)` descending               | `ActivityLog.(gardenId, seasonId, date)`          |
| Plant profile autocomplete  | Fetch PlantProfiles by `(userId, name)` prefix                      | `PlantProfile.(userId, name)`                     |
| Notes for an area           | Fetch Notes by `(gardenId, seasonId, targetType: "area", targetId)` | `Note.(gardenId, seasonId, targetType, targetId)` |
| History comparison          | Load two Seasons' plantings and logs side by side                   | Same compound indexes, two queries                |


---

## 5. Data Lifecycle


| Event                       | Effect                                                                                                                                                               |
| --------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **User deletes account**    | All user data is deleted: memberships, plant profiles, owned gardens (cascade), and files from Object Storage. Other members lose access to gardens they didn't own. |
| **Garden is deleted**       | All child entities are cascade-deleted: areas, seasons, plantings, logs, tasks, notes, files. GardenMemberships for this garden are removed.                         |
| **Season is archived**      | `isActive` set to `false`. Data is retained for historical browsing. A new active season is created.                                                                 |
| **PlantProfile is deleted** | Existing plantings retain the denormalized `plantName`. The `plantProfileId` reference becomes a dangling ref (treated as null on read).                             |
| **Area is deleted**         | Plantings referencing this area become orphaned. The UI should warn before deletion if active plantings exist.                                                       |
| **File is deleted**         | MongoDB record removed; corresponding S3 object deleted. References from logs/notes become dangling (rendered as "attachment removed").                              |


---

## 6. Schema Evolution

Because MongoDB is schemaless, field additions are straightforward — new optional fields are simply absent in older documents and handled with defaults at the application layer.

**Guidelines:**

- New fields must be optional or have a default value to avoid breaking existing documents.
- Mongoose schema definitions serve as the source of truth for document shape.
- Any rename or removal of a field requires a one-time migration script (stored in `backend/migrations/`).
- The repository interface shields the service layer from schema changes — if a field is renamed in MongoDB, only the repository implementation changes.
